commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3fc6f29db0575ab506178bdda245145f49d865a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the heartbeat transcript artifact filter to remove trailing unrecognized assistant heartbeat output and adds focused tests around trimming and preservation.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +24. Total +29 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.5.22 preserve trailing unrecognized heartbeat assistant output, and the PR adds tests for trimming that shape. I did not establish a live DeepSeek/Stepfun or Telegram failing run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review because the patch addresses the stale trailing-context shape but also changes prior plain-text alert preservation and lacks real behavior proof.

target.title: fix: trim trailing heartbeat context when no user message follows
target.body:
## Summary

Models that do not produce a clean `HEARTBEAT_OK` token (e.g. DeepSeek V4) leave their heartbeat response in the transcript. The next real user message is then appended after this stale context, causing the agent to misidentify it as a heartbeat continuation.

`resolveHeartbeatArtifactSpanEnd` now trims unrecognized assistant output when the heartbeat pair is the trailing turn. It only preserves non-standard heartbeat responses when a subsequent user message proves the user interacted with them.

Closes #85614

## Verification

Behavior addressed: first user message after heartbeat poll misidentified as heartbeat continuation
Real environment tested: macOS local, Node 22
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts
Evidence after fix: 36/36 tests pass including 3 new tests for the trailing heartbeat scenario
Observed result after fix: trailing heartbeat context is trimmed; user-interacted heartbeat context is preserved
What was not tested: live multi-model heartbeat with DeepSeek V4 (requires live deployment)
target.author: sebtardif
